contract_traceability: research/ACTION_SPACE_INCREMENTAL_RL.md — Lifecycle contract with novelty claims, baseline requirements, evaluation requirements, and blocking unknowns
architect/ACTION_INCREMENTAL_RL_ARCHITECTURE.md — Architecture design with traceability table mapping each contract item to implementation decisions
architect/model_config.py, components.py, agent.py, train.py — Implementation files
coder/networks.py, agent.py, env_wrapper.py, train.py, smoke_test.py — Executable implementation with 22 smoke tests passing
validator/test_model.py — Comprehensive test suite (Layer 1-2): shape, gradient, RL correctness, numerics, benchmarks
validator/run_ablations.py — Ablation runner (Layer 3): 14 single-field config ablations
validator/profile_model.py — Profiling script (Layer 4): forward, train, expansion with FLOP estimates
baseline_status: retrain_from_scratch: IMPLEMENTED (TODO) — train.py supports --env cartpole for demoware; custom environment not yet implemented; ablation runner provides proxy evaluation only
fixed_action_oracle: NOT_IMPLEMENTED — No script that trains with full action set from beginning (requires pre-built custom environment)
zero_initialization: IMPLEMENTED (ablation) — ablation runner includes 'no_knn_init' (k_nn=0) which uses random init as a proxy for zero-init
gas_adapted_baseline: NOT_IMPLEMENTED — Farquhar et al. parent-child Q-decomposition requires known hierarchy; not applicable to unexpected expansion setting

grounded: Nested action hierarchy enables provable monotonic value transfer (Farquhar et al., ICML 2020)
Page-Hinkley drift detection enables on-the-fly detection (de la Rosa et al., 2026)
Action embeddings via InfoNCE can generalize across action-set sizes (Sinii et al., 2023)
Progressive networks prevent catastrophic forgetting at cost of linear parameter growth (Rusu et al., 2016)
hypothesis: Similarity-based Q-transfer from known to new actions in embedding space provides better-than-random initialization — tested via 'no_knn_init' ablation in ablation runner
Factorized Q(s,a) = f_Q(φ(s), e_a) minimizes interference — tested via 'dueling_q' and baseline comparison in ablation runner
Freezing the base encoder during initial fine-tuning prevents catastrophic forgetting — tested via 'no_freeze_encoder' ablation
Softmax denominator shift from adding new actions causes measurable degradation — dueling variant with known-action-only centering implemented as mitigation
TODO_unverified: No deep RL method handles truly unexpected action-space expansion without predefined hierarchy or tabular state space — novelty claim pending empirical validation against baselines
No standardized Gymnasium benchmark exists for action-space incremental RL — custom DAVE-game environment raises NotImplementedError
The quantitative impact of softmax denominator shift on old-action logits is unmeasured — dueling centering mitigation implemented but no measurement script
k-NN initialization quality for semantically novel actions (e.g., 'shoot' vs. movement) — core hypothesis could be falsified if embeddings lack structure
Full baseline comparisons (retrain-from-scratch, fixed-action-oracle) not yet run due to missing custom environment

evidence: The factorized Q architecture with k-NN embedding initialization differs from GAS (requires predefined hierarchy), morphin (tabular), and Headless-AD (bandit/MDP only)
Progressive unfreezing schedule (Phase 1-2-3) is novel for action-space expansion specifically (not task-incremental learning)
Optimistic exploration bonus combined with embedding interpolation is a non-obvious combination
concerns: No empirical evidence yet that the method works — all tests are on synthetic/proxy tasks
Could be seen as an engineering combination of existing techniques (GAS embeddings + progressive nets + DQN) rather than a fundamentally new algorithm
The centroid-based k-NN initialization uses all known embeddings equally — no semantic distinction between actions

evidence: 22 smoke tests covering all components, forward shapes, gradients, expansion, freeze schedule
39 pytest tests covering shapes, gradients, RL correctness, numerics, and synthetic benchmarks
14 ablation conditions covering embedding init, optimizer bonus, freeze schedule, Q-form, aux loss, embedding dimension, similarity metric
Profiling script for forward pass, training step, and expansion cost
Both q_form variants tested (factorized and dueling_factorized)
concerns: No custom Gymnasium environment implemented — DAVE-game, continuous control, and goal-conditioned scenarios raise NotImplementedError
No full training-based evaluation — proxy metric uses random states, not environment interaction rewards
Retrain-from-scratch baseline not implemented (requires custom environment)
Fixed-action-oracle baseline not implemented (requires full action set from beginning)
No GAS-adapted baseline (not applicable but could serve as comparison if hierarchy can be post-hoc imposed)
No statistical rigor (10 seeds per condition) — all tests use single seed

evidence: Monotonicity property of nested action spaces correctly cited and applied
Factorized Q(s,a) = f_Q(φ(s), e_a) justified with decoupling argument
k-NN centroid initialization justified as minimax optimal under no semantic descriptor
Off-policy (DQN) correctly justified over on-policy for this problem setting
Progressive unfreeze schedule grounded in continual learning principles
Softmax denominator shift problem identified and dueling centering mitigation implemented
concerns: The auxiliary dynamics loss justification (creates semantically meaningful embedding space) is plausible but untested — no embedding visualization or t-SNE analysis
No formal proof that the factorized architecture preserves the monotonicity property under function approximation

evidence: Ablation runner produces comparative metrics across 14 conditions
Q-degradation metric computed before/after expansion
Expansion cooldown guard tested to prevent rapid successive expansions
concerns: No training-based results — all evaluations use synthetic random states, not actual environment rollouts
No confidence intervals or multiple-seed statistics
No comparison to retrain-from-scratch or fixed-action-oracle
No training curves (episode rewards over time)
No sensitivity analysis for key hyperparameters (k_nn values, embedding dims)

evidence: All smoke tests pass: python smoke_test.py
All pytest tests runnable with single command
Ablation runner produces JSON output: python run_ablations.py
Profiling script runnable with --mode flags
All hyperparameters documented in ModelConfig dataclass with sensible defaults
Random seeds propagated through torch.manual_seed and np.random.seed
Gradient hook (freeze mask) is torch.compile-compatible
bf16/fp16 safety noted in network forward passes
concerns: Train.py raises NotImplementedError for dave environment
Custom benchmark environment still TODO (Gap 4 from research contract)
No setup.py or requirements.txt — dependencies (gymnasium, numpy, torch) must be pre-installed
No Dockerfile or environment specification for exact reproduction

evidence: Architecture is well-documented with ASCII diagrams in the architect document
Inductive bias justification table covers 12 design choices
Research-to-architecture traceability table maps 10 contract items to implementation
Implementation risk flags documented with likelihood, impact, and mitigations
Ablation table with 8 conditions, tier ordering, and expected metric movements
concerns: No paper draft or abstract
No literature review section written (only gap analysis table)
No results section — no training curves, no comparison tables
No limitations section beyond implementation risks
No discussion of broader impact or ethical considerations

blocking_gaps: benchmark_not_beaten: No custom Gymnasium environment (DAVE-game analogue, continuous control, goal-conditioned). All three raise NotImplementedError. Cannot run full training-based evaluation without this.
benchmark_not_beaten: Retrain-from-scratch baseline not implemented. Cannot claim sub-linear fine-tuning without this comparison.
benchmark_not_beaten: Fixed-action-oracle baseline not implemented. Cannot compute % total training steps saved.
benchmark_not_beaten: No GAS-adapted baseline (may not be applicable but noted as requirement in research contract).
coverage_gap: Only proxy evaluation metrics (Q stats on random states), not actual environment rewards.
coverage_gap: No multiple-seed (≥10) experiments. All tests use single seed.
coverage_gap: No embedding quality evaluation (t-SNE, cosine similarity matrix, k-NN init quality vs. random).
novelty_unverified: Core hypothesis (k-NN initialization provides better-than-random Q-values for new actions) untested on actual RL task.
claim_not_grounded: 'Sub-linear fine-tuning cost' claim cannot be validated without retrain-from-scratch comparison on actual environment.
claim_not_grounded: 'No catastrophic forgetting' claim partially tested (frozen encoder preserves old Q-values in Phase 1) but not validated through full training.

recommended_next_experiments.experiment: Implement custom Gymnasium environment (DAVE-game analogue) with expanding action sets at level transitions
Run full training with retrain-from-scratch baseline: compare total environment steps to reach equivalent performance
k-NN init vs. random init comparison on actual RL task: measure zero-shot Q(a_new) at first encounter after expansion
Run 10 seeds per condition, report mean ± std for all metrics
Embedding space analysis: t-SNE visualization, cosine similarity matrix, k-NN init quality metric
Hyperparameter sensitivity sweep: k_nn in {1, 3, 5, 10}, d_embedding in {8, 16, 64, 256}, freeze_steps in {100, 1000, 10000, 100000}
Q-degradation measurement: log old-action Q-values at fixed (state, action) pairs before/after expansion through full training
Continuous control scenario (MuJoCo with expanding action dimension)
Auxiliary dynamics loss ablation: t-SNE comparison of embedding spaces with vs. without aux loss
